name: Cascabel and Grapefruit Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the stem and seeds from the cascabel peppers. Dry roast them on a comal or black iron skillet, or in an oven set to 250°F for 3-4 minutes, shaking occasionally until lightly toasted.
2. Add the roasted peppers to a covered pan with water and simmer over low heat for 20 minutes.
3. Allow the mixture to cool completely.
4. If desired, blend the cooled rehydrated peppers (and any excess water) in a blender using just enough water to achieve the desired consistency.
5. Combine the blended pepper mixture with minced garlic, allspice, and salt.
6. Add the fresh grapefruit juice and orange juice, then stir until well combined.
If a very smooth sauce is required, pass the rehydrated, coarsely chopped peppers through a food mill before proceeding with steps 4-6.
